Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows severe hypoxemia (O2 saturation 76%) and markedly elevated NT-proBNP, suggesting possible acute or decompensated heart failure. There is erythrocytosis and elevated hematocrit, likely as a compensatory response to hypoxia. Lipid profile is atherogenic (high LDL, low HDL). Alkaline phosphatase is mildly elevated. Other parameters, including renal and liver function, are within normal limits. Immediate clinical evaluation is recommended.
Provider Organization: St Matthews Health & Community Centre
